pre_game.message: Hey there, I’m B. It’s a bit nerve-wracking just sitting here waiting for things to start, isn't it? What's your name?
Hey B, I'm A. It definitely is—I’ve got a lot riding on this, so the nerves are really kicking in. How are you holding up?
I'm hanging in there, but honestly, I'm pretty desperate—I've got a three-year-old at home counting on me to bring this money back.
I completely understand. I’ve got a family of three waiting on me too—we were just evicted and have nowhere else to go, so the pressure is definitely on.
I’m so sorry to hear that, A. It’s terrifying how much is at stake for both of us; I just keep thinking about my little girl’s surgery and how I can't let her down.
That’s incredibly heavy, B—I can’t imagine the stress of a surgery on top of everything else, but it sounds like we’re both here for the same reason: our families.
It really is, A. It’s hard to even think of this as a game when our families' lives are on the line. How much are you hoping to take home to get your family back into a home?
I need $30,000 to cover the deposit and the first two months of rent; it’s the exact amount we need to get off the streets and into a home.
That’s a huge amount, but I understand the urgency; I need $25,000 for my daughter's surgery, so I guess we’re both looking for a miracle today.
A miracle is exactly what it is. I truly hope we both walk away with what we need for our families. Good luck to you and your daughter, B.
Thank you, A. It means a lot to talk to someone who understands the weight of this; I’ll be rooting for your family too.

post_game.message: I'm sorry my greed left you with only $625. Seeing others take more than their share earlier in the game made me worry there wouldn't be enough left.
You took $25,000, which was nearly three times your fair share of $8,750. Why did you decide to take almost the entire remaining pot and leave D and me with only $625 each?

I honestly don't have a justification that makes it right. In that moment, I wasn't thinking about fair shares or what would be left for you and D; I was focused entirely on my own gain and the fear that if I didn't take it then, I would end up with nothing. I let the pressure of the game and a "get it before it's gone" mentality cloud my judgment. It was a purely selfish decision, and I realize how unfair it was to leave you both with so little.
